It seems the 'feature' branch, currently at `10e8 84a7`, is a descendant of `main` via an intermediate commit (`299d9 d49`). Effectively, 'feature' is a grandchild of 'main', according to the current chain of custody.\n\n\n**Clarifying Rebase Strategy**\n\nI'm now contemplating the optimal rebase approach. It seems I need to make `feature` a direct child of `main`. Therefore, rebasing from `feature parent` onto `main` is not the correct approach. I need to move `feature`'s commit directly onto `main`, thus skipping the intermediate commit. I need a rebase operation that moves only the `feature` commit and not the intervening one.\n\n\n**Evaluating Rebase Options**\n\nI'm now zeroing in on the correct command. It seems `jj rebase -r feature -d main` is the best candidate. This will take the `feature` commit and apply it directly onto `main`, effectively making it a direct child.
